title: Lerchia microphylla
intro: Lerchia microphylla, also known as Dwarf Lerch, is a small shrubby plant native to the Mediterranean region, belonging to the family Apiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as wounds, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Lerchia microphylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lerchia microphylla include infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from skin irritations to respiratory problems.
